predicted_impact: Fire likely to spread northwest 35-45km over the next 24 hours driven by sustained westerly winds, placing Sumatran Orangutan core nesting habitat at significant risk of direct encroachment.
compounding_factors: Species already at historic population low (~13,600 individuals)
Dry season conditions reducing moisture-based fire suppression
Confirmed recent GBIF sightings within 20km of fire perimeter
recommended_action: Alert conservation teams in North Aceh and West Kalimantan for immediate habitat monitoring and potential wildlife rescue standby.
reasoning: Fire severity (87% normalized) combined with westerly winds at 18.5 km/h projects a northeast fire spread toward documented orangutan range polygons. GBIF sighting data 14 days ago confirms active habitat use. Distance of 18.3km is within the 25km high-threat threshold per guidelines.
